2604.12832.title: Detecting and refurbishing ground truth errors during training of deep learning-based echocardiography segmentation models
2604.12832.paper_summary: Deep learning-based medical image segmentation typically relies on ground truth (GT) labels obtained through manual annotation, but these can be prone to random errors or systematic biases. This study examines the robustness of deep learning models to such errors in echocardiography (echo) segmentation and evaluates a novel strategy for detecting and refurbishing erroneous labels during model training. Using the CAMUS dataset, we simulate three error types, then compare a loss-based GT label error detection method with one based on Variance of Gradients (VOG). We also propose a pseudo-labelling approach to refurbish suspected erroneous GT labels. We assess the performance of our proposed approach under varying error levels. Results show that VOG proved highly effective in flagging erroneous GT labels during training. However, a standard U-Net maintained strong performance under random label errors and moderate levels of systematic errors (up to 50%). The detection and refurbishment approach improved performance, particularly under high-error conditions.
2604.12832.paper_summary_zh: 深度學習基礎的醫學影像分割通常依賴於通過手動標註獲得的真實標籤（GT），但這些標籤可能會受到隨機錯誤或系統性偏差的影響。這項研究檢視了深度學習模型對這些錯誤在心臟超音波（echo）分割中的穩健性，並評估了一種在模型訓練過程中檢測和修正錯誤標籤的新策略。利用CAMUS數據集，我們模擬了三種錯誤類型，然後將基於損失的GT標籤錯誤檢測方法與基於梯度方差（VOG）的方法進行比較。我們還提出了一種偽標註方法來修正可疑的錯誤GT標籤。我們評估了在不同錯誤水平下我們提出的方法的性能。結果顯示，VOG在訓練過程中標記錯誤GT標籤方面非常有效。然而，標準U-Net在隨機標籤錯誤和中等系統性錯誤（高達50%）下仍保持強勁的性能。檢測和修正方法在高錯誤條件下提高了性能。
2604.12832.author: Iman Islam et.al.
2604.12832.authors: Iman Islam, Bram Ruijsink, Andrew J. Reader, Andrew P. King
